message:
feat(abi): port openat2 + futex_waitv (modern Linux libc syscall wrappers) (bd-25xxp)

Two modern Linux libc wrappers that were the last gaps in our
modern-syscall coverage:

  - openat2(dirfd, pathname, *open_how, size) -> int
    (Linux 5.6, glibc 2.34): extended openat that takes a versioned
    `struct open_how { u64 flags; u64 mode; u64 resolve; }` so callers
    can pass RESOLVE_* policy bits unavailable to plain openat. We
    forward via libc::syscall(SYS_openat2, ...) and bridge errno;
    NULL pathname / NULL how return EFAULT.
  - futex_waitv(*waiters, nr_futexes, flags, *timeout, clockid) -> int
    (Linux 5.16, glibc 2.35): wait on multiple futexes simultaneously.
    Forwards to the existing frankenlibc_core::syscall::sys_futex_waitv
    with errno bridging. Returns the index of the woken futex on
    success. NULL waiters with positive nr_futexes returns EFAULT.

Tests: 6 abi tests covering openat2 happy-path file open + read,
EINVAL on wrong struct size, EFAULT on NULL pathname; futex_waitv
EINVAL on zero count, EFAULT on NULL waiters with positive count, and
ETIMEDOUT (or EAGAIN, depending on kernel) on a deadline already in
the past.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-25pf","title":"bd-rqn subtask: Production-set change policy gate (admission/retirement evidence)","description":"Background:

(1) openat2(dirfd, pathname, *open_how, size) -> int (Linux 5.6, glibc 2.34) — extended openat that takes a versioned struct open_how { u64 flags; u64 mode; u64 resolve; } describing flags + RESOLVE_* policy bits. Just dispatch SYS_openat2 (= 437 on x86_64) via libc::syscall. (2) futex_waitv(*waiters, nr_futexes, flags, *timeout, clockid) -> int (Linux 5.16, glibc 2.35). Wait on multiple futexes simultaneously; returns the index of the woken futex on success, -1 + errno on timeout/error. Forward to the existing frankenlibc_core::syscall::sys_futex_waitv. NULL-pointer / negative-count guards plus errno bridging.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:47:51.138356601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:47:51.138356601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Artifact round: target/profiles/20260211T025848Z/strict.","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:00:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-26xb","title":"EPIC: Apollo Accretive Value Program (Idea-Wizard 15)","description":"## Background
Accretive idea program to increase practical value for Apollo/FrankenLibC users without duplicating existing open-bead functionality.

## Design
Use `idea-wizard` methodology: broad ideation, ruthless winnow, overlap checks against all open beads, and operationalization into dependency-linked tasks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 15 additive ideas are represented as executable beads with clear non-overlap rationale.
- Each idea has concrete implementation shape, objective value signal, and closure gates.
- Dependencies are wired to existing foundations so execution is coherent and low-risk.

## Notes
Flagship set emphasizes debug velocity, confidence, explainability, and compute-pressure resilience.

## Success Criteria
All 15 idea tasks are closed with objective evidence, no unresolved overlap conflicts, and release-ready proof of accretive user value.","design":"Top-5 flagship ideas: autoreducer pipeline, change-impact oracle scheduler, compatibility SLO/certification packs, explainability workbench, counterfactual policy simulator.
Next-best-10: determinism sentinel, ABI auto-bisect, evidence tiering, shadow differential runner, spec-hole detector, synthetic workload composer, scaffold generator, tail-latency decomposition, CI risk-budget autopilot, incremental proof cache.","acceptance_criteria":"## Success Criteria
Epic closure requires all 15 ideas to be implemented or explicitly retired with replacement rationale and recorded decision evidence.\n\n### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression cases.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in this bead (strict and hardened mode where relevant).
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include: exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist artifacts (logs, diffs, reports) with deterministic naming and retention policy for triage/replay.
- Tooling hard requirement applies: use `/dp/asupersync` for deterministic orchestration/traceability and `/dp/frankentui` for deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI analysis output.","notes":"Non-overlap audit source: all 201 open beads were scanned by title/label/search before creating these tasks.

This epic is complementary to `bd-w2c3` and does not replace existing parity-closure obligations.\n\n### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set documented in bead comments/notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets documented (top 3 likely failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry documented for this bead linking logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

Tooling contract: closure requires evidence of `/dp/asupersync` orchestration/traceability integration and `/dp/frankentui` deterministic diff/snapshot analysis integration.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:56:52.756733844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:36.609936052Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:36.609716937Z","close_reason":"Flagship deliverable: code + artifacts in working tree.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accretive","apollo-value","execution","feature-parity","gap-closure","idea-wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26xb","depends_on_id":"bd-5fw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-26xb","depends_on_id":"bd-w2c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":731,"issue_id":"bd-26xb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Created via idea-wizard workflow:
- Top-5 flagship ideas selected for immediate value multipliers.
- Next-best-10 ideas captured as additive roadmap tasks.
- Each task includes mandatory unit/e2e/logging/tooling closure gates.
- Dependencies tie ideas to existing parity-closure foundations to prevent overlap drift.
","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:58:14Z"},{"id":732,"issue_id":"bd-26xb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien+Extreme uplift pass (2026-02-13): this epic is now covered by the full open-backlog EV matrix at artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.json (summary markdown: artifacts/planning/open_beads_alien_uplift.v1.md).

Execution contract applied:
- One-lever optimization waves only (profile -> prove -> implement -> verify).
- Budgeted mode + deterministic fallback trigger required per child bead.
- Graveyard route mappings and baseline comparators are pre-attached in matrix rows.
- Promotion gate: EV >= 2.0 and no cycle introduction in bv --robot-insights.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:25:06Z"}]}
@@ -1071,7 +1072,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-r25ks","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] abort() does not unblock SIGABRT before raising — POSIX violation, suppresses core dump","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs at lines 2297-2309, abort() raises SIGABRT via crate::signal_abi::raise without first unblocking SIGABRT.

POSIX 2008 mandates: 'If the SIGABRT signal is being held or ignored, abort() shall first unblock or unignore the SIGABRT signal.'

Current implementation:
1. fflush stdio
2. raise(SIGABRT)  -- if blocked, signal stays pending, no delivery, no termination
3. signal(SIGABRT, SIG_DFL)
4. raise(SIGABRT)  -- still blocked, still no delivery
5. sys_exit_group(134)  -- fallback, but no core dump

Consequences:
- Process exits with status 134, but no core file is generated (production debugging is impaired)
- POSIX-compliant programs that block SIGABRT around critical sections expect abort() to override the block; they will instead silently continue execution (because raise returns without delivery) -- but here we have sys_exit_group as fallback so termination is preserved
- The user's SIG_DFL re-raise behavior is bypassed when SIGABRT is blocked

Fix: Before the first raise(), call sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, ...) with a sigset containing only SIGABRT. Match glibc abort.c behavior: also clear SIG_IGN by resetting to SIG_DFL if currently SIG_IGN.

Severity: HIGH -- POSIX non-compliance + lost coredumps in production crashes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T01:54:15.710736731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T02:36:11.148658121Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T02:36:11.146362986Z","close_reason":"abort() unblocks SIGABRT before raise; POSIX-compliant; commit 64fb99b2","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r2oq","title":"Add search.h conformance fixtures and harness coverage","description":"Add dedicated tests/conformance/fixtures/search_ops.json and frankenlibc-harness coverage for <search.h> ABI surface (hcreate/hsearch/hdestroy, tsearch/tfind/tdelete/twalk, lfind/lsearch, insque/remque), following existing conformance fixture patterns and validating schema/spec coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T21:47:12.767248867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:49:35.833569558Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:49:35.831673850Z","close_reason":"Added explicit search.h fixture coverage and fixed host-parity ABI behavior for hsearch/remque","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r3qc","title":"syscall: pidfd_send_signal invalid flags should not override invalid pidfd","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 1), so invalid pidfd still dominates bad flags. Add ABI regression coverage to freeze that precedence contract exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:57:01.993657584Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:58:57.900227304Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:58:57.897016719Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_send_signal(-1, 0, NULL, 1) behavior: invalid flags do not override invalid pidfd, so the veneer still reports exact EBADF; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-rcrw7","title":"PORT: mq_clocksend + mq_clockreceive (clockid_t variants of POSIX mq_timed*)","description":"POSIX mq_clocksend(mqdes, msg, len, prio, clockid, abstime) and mq_clockreceive(mqdes, msg, len, *prio, clockid, abstime) — clockid_t variants of mq_timedsend/receive added in glibc 2.34. Dispatch on clockid: CLOCK_REALTIME forwards directly to mq_timedsend/mq_timedreceive (which already use the realtime kernel ABI). CLOCK_MONOTONIC converts the supplied monotonic abstime into a realtime abstime via "realtime_abs = realtime_now + (mono_abs - mono_now)" then forwards. Other clockids return -1 with errno=EINVAL. NULL abstime forwards as-is (means "block forever" per POSIX).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:37:11.468866720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:37:11.468866720Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-rcrw7","title":"PORT: mq_clocksend + mq_clockreceive (clockid_t variants of POSIX mq_timed*)","description":"POSIX mq_clocksend(mqdes, msg, len, prio, clockid, abstime) and mq_clockreceive(mqdes, msg, len, *prio, clockid, abstime) — clockid_t variants of mq_timedsend/receive added in glibc 2.34. Dispatch on clockid: CLOCK_REALTIME forwards directly to mq_timedsend/mq_timedreceive (which already use the realtime kernel ABI). CLOCK_MONOTONIC converts the supplied monotonic abstime into a realtime abstime via "realtime_abs = realtime_now + (mono_abs - mono_now)" then forwards. Other clockids return -1 with errno=EINVAL. NULL abstime forwards as-is (means "block forever" per POSIX).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:37:11.468866720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:42:15.744702584Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:42:15.742594619Z","close_reason":"shipped: mq_clocksend + mq_clockreceive with CLOCK_REALTIME passthrough + CLOCK_MONOTONIC abstime rebasing + EINVAL guard for unknown clockids; 4 abi tests including round-trip on both clocks and ETIMEDOUT path","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rcw4p","title":"syscall: pidfd_getfd self pidfd with invalid targetfd should return EBADF exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for pidfd_getfd(self_pidfd, -1, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-targetfd contract on a valid pidfd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:51.342051427Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:23.342856887Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:23.339853631Z","close_reason":"pidfd_getfd(self, -1, 0) → EBADF pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rdxo4","title":"PORT: __cxa_vec_new/new2/new3 + __cxa_vec_delete/delete2/delete3 + __cxa_vec_cctor","description":"Add the Itanium C++ ABI vector new/delete/copy-ctor family (7 entries) on top of our existing __cxa_vec_ctor/dtor primitives. (1) __cxa_vec_new(count, size, padding, ctor, dtor) -> *mut c_void: malloc(count*size + padding), if padding > 0 stash count at the start of the padding so __cxa_vec_delete can recover it, run ctor on each element, return array (= raw + padding). (2)/(3) __cxa_vec_new2/3 with custom alloc/dealloc functions. (4) __cxa_vec_delete(array, size, padding, dtor): if array NULL no-op; recover raw + count from padding, run dtor reverse, free raw. (5)/(6) __cxa_vec_delete2/3 with custom dealloc (delete2 takes ptr-only, delete3 takes ptr+size). (7) __cxa_vec_cctor(dest, src, count, size, ctor, dtor): copy-constructor variant. NULL ctor/dtor must be tolerated. count*size+padding uses checked arithmetic; overflow returns NULL. Implementations route through our crate::malloc_abi::malloc/free except where the user supplied custom allocators.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:15:56.551953229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:20:51.929936674Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:20:51.927873908Z","close_reason":"shipped: 7 cxa vector new/delete/cctor primitives w/ count-in-padding stashing + 8 abi tests including round-trip and custom alloc/dealloc, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-reax","title":"EPIC: Replace critical stubs with real implementations or host delegation","description":"The comprehensive audit identified several categories of stub/placeholder implementations:

1. SAFE NO-OP STUBS (~50 functions): Return 0/-1/NULL without doing real work. These are intentional for features we don't support (profiling, NSS, netgroups). LOW priority — they don't crash, just silently no-op.

2. HOST CALL-THROUGH DEBT (~70 symbols): Functions that delegate to host glibc via RTLD_NEXT. These work correctly but represent symbols we haven't implemented natively yet. LOW priority — they work fine via delegation.

3. CORE TERMIOS STUBS: tcgetattr/tcsetattr/tcflush/tcflow in frankenlibc-core return -1. NOT a real issue because the ABI layer (termios_abi.rs) uses raw ioctl syscalls directly and never calls these core stubs.

4. RPC SUBSYSTEM (~68 stubs): Return safe defaults. MEDIUM priority — RPC is legacy but some programs still use it.

5. ELF LOADER INCOMPLETE: Only Phase-1 relocations; IFUNC deferred. MEDIUM priority — affects shared library loading.

RATIONALE: Focus on items that break real programs first. The setjmp/longjmp stubs were already fixed (now delegate to host). The on_exit handler gap was already fixed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PlumCove","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:55.983549992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:38.984372338Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:47:38.983957473Z","close_reason":"Both children closed: bd-reax.1 (RPC host delegation) and bd-reax.2 (IFUNC relocation). Remaining categories: safe no-op stubs (intentional for unsupported features like profiling/NSS/netgroups), host call-through (working via delegation), and core termios stubs (ABI layer uses raw ioctl, never calls core stubs). No critical stubs remain.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
